cards.body: Short, practical courses for hotel marketers, revenue managers, Partners, and builders learning the closed loop from content to confirmed booking.

cards.body: Ongoing articles on direct distribution, social commerce, partner attribution, booking engines, AI content for hotels, and travel-commerce technology.

cards.body: Direct booking strategy, OTA economics, channel mix, Booking Engine setup, WinkLinks, WinkSocial, Traveliko, and partner channel control.

cards.body: How to build bookable storefronts, use Social Links, understand attribution, earn on confirmed bookings, and work with hotels directly.

cards.title: Developers and platforms
cards.body: How search, booking, payment, attribution, webhooks, and SDKs fit into the Wink platform layer.

cards.title: Win more bookings without giving the guest relationship back to an OTA.
cards.body: Channel mix, direct booking benchmarks, booking economics, rate strategy, and owned checkout.

cards.title: Turn travel attention into bookable demand.
cards.body: Social Links, WinkLinks, partner attribution, content-to-booking paths, and campaign examples.

items.question: What is Wink Resources?
items.answer: Wink Resources is the learning hub for direct hotel distribution, travel social commerce, partner demand, hotel technology, and the booking economics behind Wink.

items.question: Who is Wink Resources for?
items.answer: It is for hotels, partners, platform teams, developers, agencies, and travel-commerce operators who want to understand how demand moves from content to confirmed booking.

items.question: What is the State of Direct Hotel Distribution?
items.answer: It is Wink's flagship research report on direct booking, OTA dependence, social commerce, partner channels, and hotel technology. Public statistics should only appear once the research is approved.

items.question: Is Wink Academy free?
items.answer: Yes. Wink Academy is positioned as free practical training for hotel marketers, revenue managers, partners, and technical teams learning direct distribution and social commerce.

items.question: Where should developers go?
items.answer: Developers can start in Resources for context, then move to Developers for documentation, SDKs, API concepts, webhooks, and platform integration paths.

fields.heading: Ready to make hotel demand bookable?
fields.body: Start with the route that matches your team, then move into a focused demo or sign-up flow.
